description: Pokemon competitive battle - game-level vocabulary, shared across all formats (singles, doubles, etc.).

fields.pokemon: the Pokemon involved for single-Pokemon events, if visible
fields.hp_percent: for single-Pokemon events, that Pokemon's HP percent if visible, else null
fields.turn: field_state: the current turn number if shown, else null
fields.trick_room: field_state: true if Trick Room is active, else false
fields.tailwind: field_state: which side has Tailwind - player/opponent/both/none
fields.screens: field_state: active screens + side, e.g. 'player Reflect', else none
fields.field_status: field_state: statuses + stat stages on active Pokemon, e.g. 'opp Incineroar -1 Atk; my Flutter Mane burned'

notes: POKEMON specifics: read the battle text box - 'X used [Move]!' = move_used; 'A critical hit!' = critical_hit; 'It's super effective!' = super_effective_hit; 'X fainted!' = pokemon_fainted. A type-changing ABILITY (Protean/Libero, shown as 'X's Protean!') is item_or_ability_activated, NOT terastallized; only use terastallized when the crystalline Tera crown or 'Terastallized into the [type] type' is shown. A single Pokemon fainting is ALWAYS 'pokemon_fainted', NEVER 'battle_end'. PER-TURN STATE (important for coaching): emit one 'field_state' per turn capturing a full snapshot - turn number, the four active Pokemon with HP, weather, terrain, trick_room (true/false), tailwind (which side), screens, and field_status (statuses + stat stages on the active Pokemon). Read the field banners and status icons literally.
